title: cicuta resinifera
intro: Cicuta resinifera, also known as American Water Hemlock or Poison Hemlock, is a highly toxic and deadly plant species belonging to the carrot family. Historically, it has been used for its medicinal properties, despite its toxicity, to treat various health issues. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems contain a compound called coniine, which has been used to treat conditions such as epilepsy, rheumatism, and even snake bites. However, herbal preparations of Cicuta resinifera are highly dangerous and not recommended, as even small amounts can be fatal, and should only be handled by experienced professionals in a controlled environment.
